content: I've been thinking about learning a new skill since I moved to Berlin last month. The house renovation is taking forever though - I'm living in chaos!
Welcome to Berlin! Moving and renovating at the same time sounds incredibly stressful. What kind of skills were you considering learning? Something to distract from the renovation chaos?

Exactly! I work as a technology journalist, so I'm on the computer all day. I want something completely different - maybe something more tactile. I've always loved mechanical keyboards, so maybe electronics?

That's a great idea! Building or modifying mechanical keyboards could be perfect - it combines your interest in technology with hands-on work. Plus, you'd end up with a custom keyboard for your home office.
I love that idea! My current keyboard is falling apart anyway. I've been watching some YouTube videos about keyboard building, but I'm worried I'll mess it up. I'm not exactly a DIY expert.

Everyone starts somewhere! There are beginner kits that come with everything you need and step-by-step instructions. Since you enjoy crossword puzzles, you might like the problem-solving aspect of it.
That's true - I do love figuring things out. I've also been trying to learn German since moving here, but it's harder than I expected. Maybe I could label my keyboard keys in German as I learn?
What a clever way to combine both learning goals! You could even join some Berlin-based maker communities - I bet there are workshops where you could get help and meet people.

That's a great suggestion. I've been meaning to get more involved in the local community. Between the renovation and work, I haven't had much time for my birdwatching though. Maybe I could build something for that too?

Absolutely! You could build a bird feeder or bird house with some basic woodworking skills. It would give you a break from the house renovation and let you enjoy your birdwatching hobby from your new home.

Perfect! I think I'll start with the keyboard since I need it for work, then move on to bird-related projects. Thanks for the encouragement - I was feeling overwhelmed but now I'm excited to try something new!
